business_goals: Provide an Event framework to be used by an application
Handle and process events through component interaction
Allow application components to utilize the framework services
technical_constraints: Must handle system termination via STOP handler
Must maintain system in idle state until input is received

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: Fully developed architecture with class diagrams, component descriptions, and defined component interactions.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: Can the components of the architecture be reused for future projects?
Can the framework be expanded in the future to accommodate a new application or new components?
Will the system handle any input given by the user and handle invalid input?
Is the architecture consistent in behavior?
Can any new application-specific functionality be added to the architecture?
Can the system be modified in short time and cost-effective manner?
Does the architecture perform its event-processing task properly?

hardware_view_description: System accepts input via Command Line Interface (CLI)
Single processor execution implied (sequential event processing)
runtime_constraints: Events must be dispatched in a First-Come-First-Served (FIFO) pattern
System must maintain an 'idle wait' state until input is received
Application logic is embedded within framework components

quantitative_model_parameters.performance_proxy: Number of components involved in a transaction
quantitative_model_parameters.transaction_speed: Qualitative (Slow due to high number of components involved)

questions: Does the system allow unauthorized access?
Does the architecture portray data confidentiality?
Does the architecture process any task in the fastest possible speed?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: No unauthorized access to the system
All operations are processed in fastest possible speed
Any failure should be followed by immediate recovery
User errors in using system should be handled
New demands on system functionality should be accommodated
Major parts of the framework should be re-usable for future architectures
Framework modification is cost-effective, fast and in minimal time
Components in framework interact in a coordinated manner
Framework expansion is possible to allow for more sophisticated options
Implementation allows for execution in different environments
Proper data encapsulation and secure data structures
Flexible implementation using other programming languages
Overall consistent behavior expected from the architecture
Framework should be complete, clear and perform exactly as required
Hook up multiple applications to the framework at same time
Change interface from command-driven to window-based

architectural_approaches.description: The framework utilizes a decoupled event-based model where events are produced by inputs (CLI), stored in a FIFO queue, and dispatched to handlers. This decouples the event source from the execution logic.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Enables the core requirement of processing asynchronous events through component interaction.
The use of a FIFO queue ensures that events are processed in the order received, providing predictable system behavior.

architectural_approaches.description: The system is structured as a reusable core framework that provides event dispatching and lifecycle management (STOP handlers), allowing application-specific components to be plugged in.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Allows developers to add new application-specific functionality without altering the underlying event-processing engine.
Supports the business goal of being a reusable framework for different types of event-processing applications.

architectural_approaches.description: A single point of control manages the transition between the 'idle wait' state and the active processing state, routing all events to appropriate component handlers.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Provides a unified and consistent mechanism for handling all inputs, invalid data, and system termination (STOP handler).

children.scenario_description: A developer adds a new application-specific event handler without modifying the core framework.
The FIFO queue correctly sequences events during a burst of 1,000 CLI inputs without data loss.
The centralized dispatcher routes an event to its handler in less than 50ms.
All invalid CLI inputs are processed through a unified 'Invalid Input' handler following the same lifecycle as standard events.

scenarios.scenario_text: A developer adds a new application-specific event handler without modifying the core framework.
The FIFO queue correctly sequences events during a burst of 1,000 CLI inputs without data loss.
The centralized dispatcher routes an event to its handler in less than 50ms.
All invalid CLI inputs are processed through a unified 'Invalid Input' handler following the same lifecycle as standard events.

scenario_reference.text: A developer adds a new application-specific event handler without modifying the core framework.

analysis_records.stimulus: New functional requirement requiring a new event type
analysis_records.response_measure: Core framework source code remains unchanged; only new handler and registration logic are added.
analysis_records.reasoning: The plugin pattern decouples the execution engine from the business logic. By using a common interface, the dispatcher can invoke handlers without knowing their implementation details.

non_risks.rationale: The use of a plugin architecture specifically targets this scenario, making the addition of handlers a routine task with low impact on core stability.

scenario_reference.text: The FIFO queue correctly sequences events during a burst of 1,000 CLI inputs without data loss.

analysis_records.response_measure: Events are processed in the exact order received; 0% message loss.
analysis_records.reasoning: A single-threaded dispatcher consuming from a FIFO queue inherently guarantees sequential processing, as there is no concurrency to cause race conditions or out-of-order execution.

risks.rationale: If the burst of 1,000 inputs exceeds the memory allocated for the FIFO queue before the dispatcher can clear them, data loss will occur.

non_risks.rationale: The single-threaded model ensures that event N+1 never starts before event N finishes, providing high confidence in ordering.

scenario_reference.text: The centralized dispatcher routes an event to its handler in less than 50ms.

analysis_records.response_measure: Latency from queue egress to handler entry < 50ms
analysis_records.reasoning: A direct mapping in a centralized dispatcher provides O(1) or O(log n) lookup for handlers, which is well within the 50ms threshold for local CLI applications.

risks.rationale: Because the dispatcher is single-threaded, if a single handler takes a long time to execute, it blocks all subsequent events in the queue, potentially violating the 50ms latency for later events.
